Restaurant.title: Restaurant
Restaurant.description: Represents a restaurant managed by the system, including its profile, subscription plan, and operational status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Restaurant entity.

name.description: The official name of the restaurant.

plan.description: The subscription plan associated with the restaurant (e.g., 'Free', 'Standard', 'Premium').

status.description: The operational status of the restaurant (e.g., 'Active', 'Inactive', 'Suspended').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the restaurant record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the restaurant record was last updated.

RestaurantUser.title: RestaurantUser
RestaurantUser.description: Links an external user account (e.g., from Firebase Authentication) to a specific restaurant and defines their role within that restaurant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RestaurantUser entity, typically corresponding to the external authentication system's user ID (e.g., Firebase Auth UID).

role.description: The role of the user within the restaurant (e.g., 'Admin', 'Waiter').

isActive.description: Indicates whether the user account is currently active for this restaurant.

MenuItemCategory.title: MenuItemCategory
MenuItemCategory.description: Organizes menu items into logical categories for display in the digital menu.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItemCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the menu item category (e.g., 'Appetizers', 'Main Courses', 'Drinks').

order.description: The display order of the category within the restaurant's menu.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents a single item available on the restaurant's digital menu.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

printSectorId.description: Reference to PrintSector, indicating where this item's order should be printed. (Relationship: PrintSector 1:N MenuItem)

name.description: The name of the menu item.

description.description: A detailed description of the menu item, possibly generated by AI.

price.description: The current selling price of the menu item.

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the menu item is currently available for ordering.

imageUrl.description: Optional URL for an image representing the menu item.

PrintSector.title: PrintSector
PrintSector.description: Defines a logical sector within a restaurant for directing order items to specific printers (e.g., 'Kitchen', 'Bar').

id.description: Unique identifier for the PrintSector entity.

name.description: The name of the print sector (e.g., 'Cozinha', 'Bar').

Printer.title: Printer
Printer.description: Represents a physical printer configured to receive order items from a specific print sector.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Printer entity.

name.description: A friendly name for the printer (e.g., 'Kitchen Printer 1').

ipAddress.description: The IP address of the network printer.

port.description: Optional port number for connecting to the printer.

Table.title: Table
Table.description: Represents a physical table within a restaurant, used for associating customer orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Table entity.

qrCodeUrl.description: Optional URL for the QR code that grants access to the table's digital menu and ordering.

capacity.description: The seating capacity of the table.

status.description: The current status of the table (e.g., 'Free', 'Occupied', 'Cleaning').

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer who has placed orders, primarily for historical tracking and repeat orders, especially for non-table orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

contactNumber.description: The customer's contact number, often used for WhatsApp interactions.

notes.description: Any specific notes or preferences for this customer.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's order, containing details such as status, total amount, and associated table or customer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

tableId.description: Optional reference to Table if the order is associated with a specific table. (Relationship: Table 1:N Order)

customerId.description: Optional reference to Customer if the order is associated with a specific customer (e.g., WhatsApp orders). (Relationship: Customer 1:N Order)

waiterUserId.description: Optional reference to RestaurantUser (waiter) who created or managed the order. (Relationship: RestaurantUser 1:N Order)

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Open', 'Preparing', 'Ready', 'Completed', 'Canceled').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

notes.description: Any additional notes or special requests for the entire order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order was last updated.

closedAt.description: Optional timestamp when the order was completed or canceled.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single item within an order, including quantity, price at time of order, and item-specific notes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

quantity.description: The quantity of the menu item included in this order item.

priceAtOrder.description: The price of the menu item at the moment it was added to the order.

notes.description: Any specific notes or modifications for this individual item (e.g., 'no onions', 'well done').

status.description: The status of this specific order item (e.g., 'Pending', 'Preparing', 'Ready', 'Delivered', 'Canceled').

definition.description: Top-level collection for all restaurant profiles. The `restaurantId` serves as the document ID. Access is primarily controlled by users with roles associated with this `restaurantId`.

Stores a user's specific role and active status within a particular restaurant. The `userId` (Firebase Auth UID) and `restaurantId` from the entity schema are captured by the path parameters. This structure is critical for DBAC, allowing security rules to efficiently fetch a user's role for a restaurant.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Restaurant this role applies to.

definition.description: Organizes menu items into categories for a specific restaurant. Includes `restaurantId` in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Restaurant this category belongs to.

params.name: menuItemCategoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu item category.

definition.description: Represents a single menu item available in a specific restaurant's digital menu. Includes `restaurantId` in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Restaurant this menu item belongs to.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu item.

definition.description: Defines logical print sectors within a specific restaurant for directing order items. Includes `restaurantId` in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Restaurant this print sector belongs to.

params.name: printSectorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the print sector.

definition.description: Represents a physical printer configured for a specific restaurant and print sector. Includes `restaurantId` in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Restaurant this printer belongs to.

params.name: printerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the printer.

definition.description: Represents a physical table within a specific restaurant. Includes `restaurantId` in the path for authorization independence. Used for customer orders via QR code.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Restaurant this table belongs to.

params.name: tableId
params.description: The unique identifier of the table.

definition.description: Stores customer details for a specific restaurant, primarily for historical tracking and repeat orders. Includes `restaurantId` in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Restaurant this customer belongs to.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

definition.description: Represents a customer's order placed at a specific restaurant. Includes `restaurantId` in the path for authorization independence. Can be linked to a `tableId` or `customerId`.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Restaurant this order belongs to.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

definition.description: Represents a single item within an order, nested under the `/orders/{orderId}` path. Inherits `restaurantId` implicitly from its parent order's path for authorization.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Restaurant the parent order belongs to.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for 'Comanda Digital' prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Aware Security Rules (QAPs) by consistently denormalizing `restaurantId` into collection paths and leveraging a dedicated structure for user roles. All restaurant-specific data is organized as subcollections under `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/`. This ensures that access rules for any restaurant-related document (e.g., menu items, tables, orders) can directly check the `restaurantId` from the document's path, eliminating the need for expensive `get()` operations on parent documents. For user authorization, a top-level collection `users/{userId}/restaurantRoles/{restaurantId}` is introduced. This path-based ownership for user roles allows Firestore security rules to efficiently retrieve a user's specific role for a given restaurant using `get(/users/$(request.auth.uid)/restaurantRoles/$(restaurantId)).data.role`, directly associating the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) with their permissions within a specific restaurant. This approach makes rules simple, atomic, and easy to debug. 

QAPs are supported because read operations for collections like `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/menuItems` can be secured by rules that ensure the `request.auth.uid` has an appropriate role in that `restaurantId` (for staff) or by allowing public read access if the request originates from a QR code scan for a particular `restaurantId` without requiring authentication. By segregating data based on `restaurantId` and explicit user roles, all documents within a given collection share a homogeneous security posture, making `list` operations inherently secure.
